questions.question: Differentiate between LAN, MAN, and WAN based on geographic coverage, speed, and ownership.
keyPoints: LAN: small area (building/campus), high speed (100 Mbps-10 Gbps), privately owned
MAN: city/metro area, moderate speed (100 Mbps-10 Gbps), owned by service providers
WAN: global/national coverage, lower speed, owned by telecom companies
Key differences: scale, speed decreases with area, latency increases with scale

questions.question: Explain the working of Token Ring protocol in Ring Topology.
keyPoints: Token is a small frame that circulates on the ring
Only device holding token can transmit data
Prevents data collisions as only one station transmits at a time
Token passes from one station to next in sequence
If token is lost or station fails, token regeneration process occurs

questions.question: Compare Client-Server and Peer-to-Peer network architectures.
keyPoints: Client-Server: centralized server manages resources, clients request services
P2P: decentralized, all nodes equal, share resources directly
Client-Server: better security and control, scalable, single point of failure
P2P: fault tolerant, no server cost, slower for centralized access
Examples: Client-Server (web browsing), P2P (BitTorrent)

questions.question: Describe the advantages and disadvantages of Star Topology.
keyPoints: Advantages: easy to install, manage, detect faults; one cable failure affects only one device
Disadvantages: hub/switch failure affects entire network; requires more cable than bus
Most common topology in modern networks
Scalable - easy to add/remove devices

questions.question: What is the difference between simplex, half-duplex, and full-duplex transmission modes? Give examples of each.
keyPoints: Simplex: one-way communication only (keyboard to computer, TV broadcasting)
Half-Duplex: two-way but not simultaneous (walkie-talkies, CB radio)
Full-Duplex: simultaneous two-way (telephone, video conferencing)
Determines bandwidth utilization and communication efficiency

questions.question: Explain the concept of Hybrid Topology with an example.
keyPoints: Combines two or more different topologies
Star-Bus: star-configured clusters connected via bus backbone
Star-Ring: individual rings connected to a central hub
Inherits advantages of constituent topologies
More complex to design and manage

questions.question: Discuss Mesh Topology and its types with applications.
keyPoints: Every device connects to multiple other devices
Full Mesh: n(n-1)/2 connections; expensive but highly reliable
Partial Mesh: some devices have multiple connections; practical for large networks
High redundancy and fault tolerance
Used in backbone networks, military communications, internet infrastructure

questions.question: With the help of a diagram, explain the working of Bus Topology. Discuss its advantages, disadvantages, and the role of terminators.
keyPoints: Diagram showing single backbone cable with devices connected via drop lines
Data transmission in both directions from sender to all devices on bus
Terminators absorb signals at both ends to prevent reflection
Advantages: simple, inexpensive, easy to implement, requires less cable
Disadvantages: limited cable length, limited devices, single point of failure, traffic congestion
Collision detection important (CSMA/CD in Ethernet)

questions.question: Explain various types of computer networks classified by scale (PAN, LAN, MAN, WAN, CAN, BAN). Give their typical range, speed, and an example for each.
keyPoints: PAN: personal devices, <10m, Bluetooth/UWB (480 Mbps), wearable tech
LAN: building/campus, 1-2 km, Ethernet/Wi-Fi (100 Mbps-100 Gbps)
MAN: city, 10-50 km, Fiber (100 Mbps-10 Gbps)
WAN: global, unlimited, ISP provided (1 Mbps-100 Gbps)
CAN: campus, 1-10 km, fiber backbone, university networks
BAN: body, <2m, medical sensors, health monitors
VPN creates virtual private network over public internet

questions.question: Compare and contrast Star, Ring, and Mesh topologies. Discuss their fault tolerance, cost, and suitable applications.
keyPoints: Star: central hub, easy management, hub failure = network failure, moderate cost, most common
Ring: token passing, no collisions, one break = network failure, moderate cost, legacy systems
Mesh: multiple paths, highly fault tolerant, expensive, backbone/telecom
Full mesh cost: n(n-1)/2 connections; partial mesh practical
Fault tolerance: Mesh > Star > Ring > Bus (for cable failure)
Modern networks primarily use Star Topology with hierarchical design

questions.question: What are the different classifications of computer networks? Explain each classification with suitable examples.
keyPoints: By Scale: PAN, LAN, MAN, WAN, CAN, BAN
By Architecture: Client-Server (centralized), Peer-to-Peer (decentralized)
By Transmission Mode: Simplex, Half-Duplex, Full-Duplex
By Transmission Medium: Wired (cable/fiber), Wireless (radio/infrared)
By Ownership: Public (internet), Private (intranet), VPN
By Topology: Bus, Star, Ring, Mesh, Tree, Hybrid
